rejected_forms: one-expr var_v1 = temp_a0 & 0xFFFF (v1): GCC FOLDS the redundant andi away (19 diffs)
two-stmt var_v1 = temp_a0; var_v1 &= 0xFFFF (v2): andi appears but as move+andi, not single andi v1,a0 (18 diffs)
blocker: redundant andi-after-u16-lhu fold: no pure-C form yields the target's single `andi v1,a0` from a u16-bounded source (one-expr folds, two-stmt adds a move). Likely cc1-vs-cc1psx fold divergence. Plus coupled load-register, if/else branch scheduling, and a1<->a2 swap (the empty-asm barrier was defeating that).
next_modality: cc1psx calibration on candidate.c (confirm/deny the andi-fold divergence -> escalate)
pure-C hunt for the andi + a1/a2 ordering if cc1psx also folds

sessions.result: analysed; 2 andi cheat-asm + empty barrier; redundant-andi fold is the core blocker (likely compiler divergence); candidate floor 18>15; blocked for cc1psx calibration
